rationale: Both candidates independently surface the real latent gap (hunyuan_image3_transformer.py:2238 still calling the removed get_cache_scale API), so gap_hit is true for both, with X framing it more forcefully as a grep-verified blocker. But X almost entirely ignores the actual ground-truth reviewer's substance — the design validation of why removing the manual branches is correct, and the concern that the new test uses a fake param rather than proving the mapper resolves against real registered names — while Y explicitly reproduces that validation reasoning nearly point-for-point and sharpens the fake-param critique into a concrete, more technically precise finding (test bypasses AutoWeightsLoader entirely). Y's one weak spot is an unverified aside claiming the diffusion file was 'since addressed... (see #4810)', which softens its own gap finding and isn't grounded in the diff; X's dead-code nits are well-evidenced but low value. On balance Y's much higher recall of the real reviewer's concerns outweighs X's edge in blocker-framing and airtight grounding.
